column11:
The recent review of joint audits and scorecards across all buildings reflected a strong alignment between operational execution and client expectations. The comprehensive audit coverage has allowed us to identify cross-site trends, reinforce consistency, and ensure that every building meets the same high standard of quality.

The targeted audit approach continues to be a key driver in performance improvement. By focusing on high-impact areas and recurring findings, we’ve been able to address root causes rather than symptoms—enhancing both efficiency and long-term sustainability.

In addition, the route adjustments and on-hand training sessions implemented this quarter have proven effective in increasing visibility, optimizing labor distribution, and promoting team accountability. These actions have not only improved daily execution but also strengthened our overall readiness for audits and VIP visits.
column12: Consistency has been a key differentiator across all sites, reflected in both performance and communication. JLL and J&J have expressed strong satisfaction with the steady progress and visible improvements in service delivery. They remain encouraged by the team’s commitment and are supportive of continuing to push forward with new initiatives that strengthen quality, efficiency, and the overall customer experience.

LKC
Attended by Nick Perry (Site Lead), Ericka Najera (Soft Services Coordinator)
- Overall comments of the meeting were positive, we reviewed our negative survey and complaints and Nick Perry shared that he believes once we have our new service model in place we should see a decrease in the negative feedback. CBRE shared that our model was approved and our team should be receiving the PO for the new service model the week of 11/10.

Nick and Ericka commented on our site lead Fran who is always extremely helpful. We shared the compliments section and Ericka committed to sharing feedback on Fran because she said she always gets emails and texts complimenting her service. Shortly after the meeting, Ericka forwarded an email with some positive feedback on her.

We also reviewed our 4i process on To-Do's and report its. The site went mobile last month so really starting to see an increase in the numbers. Nick is very data driven and encouraged the use of the tool to show these items. SBM shared that our new Site Manager had been offered a role and is expected to start the week after Thanksgiving. Area Manager Wendy Struebing has a great relationship with this team and carried the meeting with a great tone. We are looking forward to increasing staffing and improving our overall scorecard results.

Concord
Attended by Pat Williams (Site Lead), Pricilla Nazario ( Customer Experience Supervisor) Stacy Harris (GMP Supervisor)

Overall the scorecard meeting was very positive. Our audit scores, complaints, compliments and corrections have all shown positive improvement over the last 2 months. 

We discussed recent issues reported in GMP adjacent stairwells, where the SBM team has began to complete daily spot checks and weekly audits after the weekly cleaning has been completed. These spaces have been high priority recently as the site has had high occurrences with Bug Findings and these stairwells have exterior doors. SBM will be implementing a sign off sheet to show action completed on the stairwells after cleaning has been conducted.

We also reviewed the log book corrections as we had 21 (threshold of 35). Pat Williams had called this out and we explained that these are largely internally corrected items during our log book checks after each shift. There were not any log book errors that lead to deviations last month. SBM quality team will also be on site the week of the 10th to continue to work with our teams, and we would like to continue to see this metric improve. 

CBRE had also brought up the desire to see report its submitted for areas such as conference and huddle room walls that have marks on them beyond our ability to clean. SBM agreed to partner with them on this and have sent out a 4i message as well as discussing during daily team chats and interactive audits. 

RTP
Attended by Jeff Allamon (Site Lead and Latoya Goolsby (Supervisor)

Scorecard review was extremely positive, our GMP Supervisor Mabel Beltran again received extremely positive feedback as she is a key part of the sites success. 

We discussed the upcoming shutdowns and partnering with CBRE to understand their project schedule so we can coordinate our cleaning in some areas after they are completed for best results. 

CBRE also shared that they will be issuing many ATB opportunities in December and January for continued projects on site. Site manager Bryan Tilley and Mabel both have an outstanding relationship with the CBRE team, and the 4i walk through was very positive. 

BOS - rescheduled to 11/10

column11:
During the scorecard reviews, we reviewed all KPI scores at each site, reviewed highlights and challenges from the past month, as well as a look forward to upcoming activity. 

LKC's look forward was very focused on staffing as we received a PO last month for the site expansion. We currently have 8 associates and are ramping up to 12. We currently have 1 starting the week of 1/12 and have 3 others in the onboarding process. 

Concord path forward was very focused on the upcoming FDA visits. We have been expecting a visit for the last 2 months to occur any day. The team has been actively preparing for this walk and are audit ready.
column12:
LKC - Ericka Najera - Ericka was surveyed in DEC and issued an outstanding compliment for the consistency of results and service provided at the site. When discussing opportunities, Ericka brought up a recent catered food event and said the food was left out without being disposed for too long. In the meeting we discussed notifying our team of catered events, and we can issue To-Do's to ensure the service is completed. Ericka also asked about our onboarding of new team members and we provided an update. 
 
Concord - Pat Willams, Lori Kirinch, Pricilla Nazario, Stacy Harris - In the Concord meeting, most of the client feedback was around our upcoming prep for the FDA audit. We discussed the CNC floors as they have noticed booties being dirty after walking the space. SBM recently increased our frequency from Weekly to Daily for floor scrubbing and are expecting to see a major improvement. We also discussed some of the delays we are seeing with Learning Plans being assigned to new employees, and developed a process to escalate to CBRE after 1 week.


Durham- Jef Allaman - Jeff relayed outstanding feedback for our team members, especially Mabel. Jeff recently sent an email thanking her for outstanding service. Jeff also mentioned that there are upcoming facility walks, he knows we are prepared but wanted to share that there are walks upcoming. 

BOS- Donnie Hui - Meeting was ran by Chad Boulton and Chris Francis as area manager Guillermo Morillo was out on vacation. During the meeting we reviewed shutdown projects, and Donnie stated "although the end result was where we want to be, he felt that our preparation could have been better."  SBM team has new team members as the site went live in September and we discovered some of the team needed additional training to complete the needed projects. We were able to utilize local resources to assist with training the team and completing the projects, but definitely had an opportunity to get in front of this.

We discussed the challenges related to the low CSAT scores at Takeda Bannockburn. Feedback from the QAP process was reviewed, and the regional leadership team will be engaged to support improvement efforts and help drive better performance.

column12:

CBRE has requested follow-up regarding the concerns raised at the Bannockburn site. SBM’s R&D team and account leadership are working closely with the client to ensure improved performance and achieve a passing score in the next CSAT cycle.

Mitigation actions will include conducting an in-person CSAT discussion, increasing site visits from both the R&D team and account leadership, and establishing weekly meetings to review and address any ongoing concerns.

Overall Performance Feedback:
CBRE provided very positive feedback regarding SBM’s overall performance, highlighting strong responsiveness and partnership. They also shared favorable comments about the RFP process, noting that the experience was smooth and that SBM was highly responsive throughout.

column9: Tyler Morello - Associate Director, Procurement
column11: We briefly reviewed each site's scorecard, KPI's listed, their weights, etc. but the conversation quickly turned to our upcoming Supplier Review Meeting, tentatively set for late April.  There are 4 suppliers presenting, all of them currently considered "preferred suppliers".  We will present last and he wants us to really highlight our ability to perform our services on a global level, including at the locations associated with their latest purchases of Cymabay and ArcellX.  I will start marketing and strategy calls this later this week and will forward the meeting invitation to the Review once date/time has been determined.
column12: No concerns mentioned.  He was quite positive, stating several times that he was confident that we will be given other locations to service after the SRM.
column13: I'm meeting with him in person next week to confidentially review the presentation made recently by Sodexo, who is likely our top competitor.  Meeting tentatively set for 3/17.
